mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack triggers recession expectations that reduce corporate profit growth forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals a synchronized collapse in consumer and residential investment demand, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20d returns with an accuracy exceeding 55%, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels above 200.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to halt tightening, prompting equity market repricing and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize in >50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.0, or the signal reverses direction during disinflation phases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly increases corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher quarterly earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 24-month windows, or INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20d returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payroll decline exceeding 50,000 signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers recession risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50000 in a single month
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return remains positive in more than 65% of instances following a payroll drop exceeding 50000, invalidating the recession risk premium channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined growth signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rates below 4.5% indicate a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >60% of periods where UNRATE falls below 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during inflationary regimes.
